ground_truth.title: Mission accomplished!
ground_truth.author: Trond Teigen
ground_truth.main_content: The National Library of Norway, the Norwegian Directorate for Cultural Heritage, and KulturIT were commissioned by the Norwegian Ministry of Culture and Equality to carry out a pilot project for the long-term preservation of digital museum objects using the National Library's digital preservation solution (DPS). Over the past year, the National Library and KulturIT have worked closely together to develop and test a solution for the ingest and delivery of digital museum objects, initially focusing on images. An API interface has been developed to enable communication between various data systems and the DPS, handling authentication and authorization with all data exchange governed by agreements between the National Library and the institutions that chooses to use the service. For data ingest and delivery, a data exchange platform based on the Amazon S3 protocol was tested, allowing users to transfer data without needing to understand how the data is stored within the platform. Requirements have been established for data delivered to the National Library to comply with the E-ARK standard for the structure and content of information packages. KulturIT has, in parallel, developed functionality for selecting museum objects for preservation, as well as solutions for ingesting and retrieving preserved museum objects from the National Library's digital preservation system. The solution has been tested by three of KulturIT's owner museums, and the pilot project is considered successful. A final report has been submitted to the Ministry of Culture and Equality, recommending establishing a permanent service for the long-term digital preservation of images for museums with an initial phase involving five museums.
with: The National Library of Norway, the Norwegian Directorate for Cultural Heritage, and KulturIT were commissioned by the Norwegian Ministry of Culture and Equality to carry out a pilot project for the long-term preservation of digital museum objects using the National Library's digital preservation solution (DPS).
An API interface has been developed to enable communication between various data systems and the DPS.
For data ingest and delivery, a data exchange platform based on the Amazon S3 protocol was tested.
Requirements have been established for data delivered to the National Library to comply with the E-ARK standard for the structure and content of information packages.
The solution has been tested by three of KulturIT's owner museums, and the pilot project is considered successful.
